PROFILE

Katsumi Okubo

Chairman, All Japan Seishinkai Karate Federation
Founder of ISKF, Soke of Seishin-ryu Kenpo Karate-do

Overview

He began training in karate in 1972 (Showa 47), joining the karate club at Kotoni Technical High School and later the Okinawan Kobudo Club at Tokai University (serving as team captain in 1979).
In 1977 (Showa 52), Soke Kazuo Kikuchi appointed him the youngest branch chief in Seishin-ryu history, leading the Sapporo Branch.
Alongside karate, he studied aikido during his university years, and bayonet fighting (jukendo), unarmed combat, and jujutsu during his service in the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force, earning 3rd dan certification directly from the Soke.
While still in the service, he also founded the Seishin-ryu Engaru Branch and Rumoi Branch.

After the Soke's passing, he reorganized the Sapporo Branch in 1986 (Showa 61) into the permanent dojo "Seishinkan Okubo Dojo."
He initially joined the Japan Seishin-ryu Karate-do Federation, led by the former headquarters' deputy director, but later withdrew from it and joined the Seishin-ryu Karate-do Association, a federation of Seishin-ryu affiliated groups, centering his activities in Sapporo.
In 2004 (Heisei 16), what had begun as a private neighborhood dojo was certified by Hokkaido Prefecture and registered with the Legal Affairs Bureau as the Specified Nonprofit Corporation All Japan Seishinkai.
As its branches continued to grow, he established the All Japan Seishinkai Karate Federation, and in 2011 (Heisei 23), following the opening of the first overseas branch (Sri Lanka), he founded the Seishin-ryu International Karate Federation (ISKF) as its international body.
In 2012 (Heisei 24), the Association awarded him 10th dan and the title of Soshi (succeeding as its 2nd-generation head), and the organization became fully independent.

He holds 8th dan certified by the National Bogu Karate-do Federation, 8th dan in karate-do certified by the (Foundation) Toko Association, and 10th dan certified by WUKO (the World Union of Karate-do Organizations); he was also certified as Japan Chief Instructor by FIKM, and was granted honorary 10th dan by Shito-ryu Sanshinkan International and the World Shorin-ryu Karate & Kobudo Association (USA).
He later named his style "Seishin-ryu Kenpo Karate-do," incorporating techniques and philosophies from various schools gained through his own training.
He currently serves as Soke and Chairman of ISKF, which today spans roughly 150 dojos in Japan and abroad with over 4,000 members.

Career

He served with the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force at the Engaru garrison and the Rumoi garrison. After returning to civilian life, he joined Suzuki Jihan Hokkaido Co., Ltd., where he worked in sales before going on to serve as manager of the Ebetsu sales office and the Head Office Higashi sales office, and later as head of the Head Office Sales Planning Group, where he oversaw the company's shift to IT as well as its events and advertising as a whole.
After leaving the company, he served as a director of the (Foundation) Toko Association, and today, alongside his karate activities, runs a martial arts equipment shop (Toyo Budogu).

Affiliations

Advisor to the World Uechi-ryu Karate-do Federation, Chairman of IKMA, honorary member of Shito-ryu Sanshinkan, and more.
He has also served as Advisor to the National Bogu Karate-do Federation, Advisor to the Okinawa Shorin-ryu Karate & Kobudo Federation, Director of the Hokkaido Mainichi Culture Association, and Director of the WRBBOF.
He has further served as WUKO-AD East Asia Regional Representative and Advisor to the Pan-American Karate-do Federation, among other roles.

Seishinkai

NPO All Japan Seishinkai Karate Federation

  • 2-1-27 Sakaedori, Shiroishi-ku, Sapporo
  • TEL 011-300-0048（Mon–Fri 13:00–16:00）
  • FAX 011-300-0094
